title: leucanthemum vulgare subsp. aligulatum
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. aligulatum, commonly known as the Oxeye Daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Europe and western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. aligulatum include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to exploit its medicinal properties.
